summary: EFS scoping was fixed, but the OIDC trust fix introduces a duplicate `StringEquals` key that breaks the condition block and fails to enforce the intended constraints.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: EFS IAM authorization Resource was '*' and needed to be scoped to the specific file system ARN
previous_issues_status.notes: Resource is now the specific EFS file system ARN with the access point condition.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: OIDC trust policy not scoped to exact repo+branch
previous_issues_status.notes: The fix attempts to use exact `sub`, but it defines two `StringEquals` keys in the same map. In Terraform this is invalid or results in the first being overwritten, so the condition block is broken and does not reliably enforce both constraints.

new_blocking_concerns.description: Duplicate `StringEquals` keys in the Condition map cause Terraform to error or overwrite the first block, breaking the trust policy conditions.
new_blocking_concerns.why_blocking: Terraform does not allow duplicate keys in a map; this will either fail to apply or drop the `aud` constraint unintentionally. Either outcome is a deploy failure or a security gap.
new_blocking_concerns.fix: Combine both keys into a single `StringEquals` map: `{ "token.actions.githubusercontent.com:aud" = "sts.amazonaws.com", "token.actions.githubusercontent.com:sub" = "repo:${var.repo}:ref:refs/heads/main" }`.
